extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won a chaotic sprint after late crash split field, holding off SAGAN Peter who crashed earlier but rejoined
tactical_patterns: KITTEL launched exposed without leadout train but still held off SAGAN, suggesting raw power overcomes positioning chaos.
SAGAN recovered from crash inside final kilometers to still finish second, revealing exceptional restart ability under pressure.
GAVIRIA positioned on SAGAN's wheel rather than organizing own team, tactical admission of Sagan's superior sprint positioning.
NIZZOLO third despite having VAN POPPEL Danny leadout suggests support riders survive crashes better in chaotic finishes.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: won despite launching far out without leadout, demonstrates he can win messy sprints through power alone
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: crashed with 1km to go yet still finished second, elite-level crash recovery and restart speed
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: positioned seventh despite using SAGAN's wheel, failed to capitalize on world champion's slipstream
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: negative: completely disrupted by late crash positioning, finished 11th showing vulnerability when sprint organization breaks down
rider_signals.VENTURINI Clément: negative: designated sprinter caught in crash, ANDRONI team lacks depth to recover sprint positioning
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: neutral: third place salvages result but presence of VAN POPPEL Danny suggests leadout partially intact
